legislators.name: Ted Cruz
legislators.professional_background: Attorney, former Solicitor General of Texas
legislators.bio_summary: Senior Senator from Texas, served since 2013. Attorney by training.
legislators.voting_record_summary: Has voted against several public health mandates, supports medical freedom legislation.

persuadability.reasoning: Strong track record opposing vaccine mandates and public health regulations. Unlikely to shift position.

legislators.name: John Cornyn
legislators.professional_background: Attorney, former Texas Supreme Court Justice
legislators.bio_summary: Senior Senator from Texas. Known for bipartisan dealmaking on certain issues.
legislators.voting_record_summary: Mixed record on health policy. Has supported some bipartisan health bills.

persuadability.reasoning: Has shown willingness to work across the aisle on health issues. Key target for outreach.

legislators.name: Yadira Caraveo
legislators.professional_background: Pediatrician, MD from University of Colorado
legislators.bio_summary: First-term Representative, pediatrician by training. Serves on Energy and Commerce committee.
legislators.voting_record_summary: Consistent supporter of public health funding and vaccine access programs.

persuadability.reasoning: Pediatrician with medical training and committee assignment on Energy & Commerce. Strong pro-science advocate.

legislators.name: Bernie Moreno
legislators.bio_summary: First-term Senator from Ohio. Business background in automotive dealerships.
legislators.voting_record_summary: New to Senate, limited voting record on health issues.

persuadability.reasoning: New senator with limited health policy record. Business-pragmatic approach could be leveraged.

legislators.name: Buddy Carter
legislators.bio_summary: Pharmacist serving since 2015. Only pharmacist in Congress. Energy and Commerce committee member.
legislators.voting_record_summary: Mixed record — supports pharmacy access bills but has voted against some public health mandates.

persuadability.reasoning: Pharmacist with healthcare background on Energy & Commerce. Key target — medical training could be leveraged for evidence-based policy.

legislators.name: Megan Blanksma
legislators.bio_summary: Idaho House Speaker Pro Tempore. Chairs the Health & Welfare committee.
legislators.voting_record_summary: As Health & Welfare chair, has significant influence over health legislation in Idaho.

persuadability.reasoning: Health committee chair with significant legislative power. Position on science policy is not firmly established.

legislators.name: Dave McCormick
legislators.professional_background: Former hedge fund CEO, West Point graduate, PhD
legislators.bio_summary: First-term Senator from Pennsylvania. PhD holder, former CEO of Bridgewater Associates.
legislators.voting_record_summary: New to Senate. HELP committee membership gives influence over health policy.

persuadability.reasoning: HELP committee member with analytical background (PhD). New senator establishing positions — key window for influence.

legislators.name: Adam Schiff
legislators.bio_summary: First-term Senator from California. Former House member and Intelligence Committee chair.
legislators.voting_record_summary: Consistent supporter of public health legislation and science funding.

persuadability.reasoning: Strong pro-science record from House tenure. Reliable vote for public health measures.
